strategies.name: Hyperbolic parity matrix
strategies.target: Add IR tests for cosh/coshf/coshl, sinh/sinhf/sinhl, tanh/tanhf/tanhl with fneg operands and check exact transformed shape.
strategies.rationale: The change is switch-case dispatch only; easy to miss one variant or assign wrong even/odd bucket.
strategies.expected_issue: Some newly added LibFunc variants may fail to fold or fold with wrong sign/parity.

strategies.name: StrictFP blocking
strategies.target: Create strictfp calls to sinh/cosh/tanh of negated args and check no canonicalization happens.
strategies.rationale: The optimization must not run when CI is strictfp; verify new hyperbolic entries respect the existing guard.
strategies.expected_issue: Rewrite could incorrectly occur in strict FP mode.

strategies.name: NoBuiltin blocking
strategies.target: Add nobuiltin sinh/cosh/tanh calls with fneg args and check original calls remain unchanged.
strategies.rationale: LibCallSimplifier exits early on nobuiltin; ensure new paths are not reachable.
strategies.expected_issue: Rewrite could incorrectly occur on nobuiltin calls.

strategies.name: NaN sign observability stress
strategies.target: Use NaN-producing input to sinh/tanh with sign-observing follow-up ops (copysign/bitcast paths) and inspect whether rewrite is still performed.
strategies.rationale: f(-x)->-f(x) may differ for NaN sign/payload behaviors in some libm implementations and observable downstream sign usage.
strategies.expected_issue: Potential semantic mismatch for odd-function rewrite when NaN sign observability matters.

strategies.name: Asinh/atanh gap probe
strategies.target: Add asinh/atanh (and f/l variants) with fneg arg to document current non-folding behavior and catch future regressions/intent changes.
strategies.rationale: Patch says hyperbolic lib functions but only sinh/cosh/tanh families were added; asinh/atanh are odd too.
strategies.expected_issue: Coverage gap: other odd hyperbolic functions remain unsimplified.

reason_thou:
1. Fix Understanding
The patch extends symmetry canonicalization in SimplifyLibCalls. Previously optimizeSymmetric handled cos/sin/tan/erf families. Now it additionally includes cosh* as even and sinh*/tanh* as odd.

2. Formal Semantic Modeling
Before: hyperbolic libcalls were not rewritten by optimizeSymmetric.
After:
- cosh(-x) -> cosh(x)
- sinh(-x) -> -sinh(x)
- tanh(-x) -> -tanh(x)
And for even functions, existing logic also applies:
- cosh(fabs(x)) -> cosh(x)
- cosh(copysign(x,y)) -> cosh(x)
The transformation is only considered when call is not strictfp.
Concrete numeric example: x=2.0 gives sinh(-2)=-sinh(2), cosh(-2)=cosh(2), tanh(-2)=-tanh(2).

3. Assumptions Identified
- LibFunc classification for all f32/f64/f80 variants is complete and correctly bucketed by parity.
- Non-strictfp mode is sufficient legality for algebraic rewriting.
- NoBuiltin and other early guards prevent illegal rewriting contexts.
- Odd/even identities are acceptable at observable IR level (including signed zero/NaN corner behavior under chosen FP constraints).

4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
- Missing one enum variant in switch (e.g., long double variant) causes missed fold.
- Wrong parity assignment yields sign error.
- Guard regressions allow folding under strictfp or nobuiltin.
- NaN/sign-observable downstream uses may reveal behavior differences for odd rewrites.
- Patch scope may be incomplete versus intent (asinh/atanh not included).

5. Test Strategies
Use targeted mutations of existing simplify-libcalls InstCombine tests to add hyperbolic call patterns with fneg/fabs/copysign, plus strictfp and nobuiltin variants, and a NaN-sign stress case. Include checks both for expected new folds and for expected non-folds under guarded modes.

{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "args": "-S -passes=instcombine", "original_ir": "
declare double @tanh(double)

define double @tanh_negated_arg(double %x) {
  %neg = fneg double %x
  %r = call double @tanh(double %neg)
  ret double %r
}

define double @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call double @tanh_negated_arg(double 1.000000e+00)
  ret double %r
}
", "transformed_ir": "

  %1 = call double @tanh(double %x)
  %r = fneg double %1
  ret double %r
}

define double @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:

  ret double %r
}

  "thoughts": "No bug found after exhaustive Phase-2 verification across all managed tests and all proposed strategies.

Bug Triggering Analysis:
- I exercised all provided hyperbolic neg-arg patterns (double/float/fp128, fneg and fsub -0.0 forms, plus extra-use OneUse blocker form) and observed expected InstCombine rewrites:
  - odd: sinh/tanh/tanhl/sinhf/sinhl/tanhf => call on positive arg then fneg result
  - even: cosh/coshf/coshl => call on positive arg
  - even+fabs: cosh(fabs(x)) => cosh(x)
  - OneUse guard: when negated operand has extra use, rewrite does not fire.
- Guard behavior was checked repeatedly:
  - callsite nobuiltin attribute prevented simplify-libcalls rewrites as expected.
  - strictfp function attribute did NOT block these rewrites in observed transforms (rewrites still occurred), but differential testing showed no behavioral divergence for tested concrete inputs; this appears to reflect current pass behavior rather than a proven miscompile from this patch.
- NaN-sign observability stress (with copysign consumers) produced no differential output differences for tested constants.
- Asinh/atanh gap probes confirmed no new symmetric fold for these functions (unchanged behavior), i.e., coverage gap/intent mismatch but not a regression introduced by this patch.

Fix Weakness Analysis:
- The patch itself is a switch-dispatch extension in optimizeSymmetric; tests confirm newly added entries are wired correctly by parity and type-family coverage.
- Potential weakness remains around strictfp expectations (transforms still happen under strictfp-attributed functions in these checks), but this appears broader than this patch and was not shown as a concrete wrong-code bug here.
